input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.reasoningText: The user has an MSTest project with a `.testsettings` file and wants to migrate it to the modern MSTest configuration format. Let me look at their project files to understand the current setup.

data.content:
The `.testsettings` format is **legacy and ignored by MSTest v3+**. You need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file. Your settings translate as follows:

| `.testsettings` | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `TestTimeout = 30000` | `<TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>` in `<MSTest>` |
| Assembly resolution / deployment | `<DeploymentEnabled>` in `<MSTest>` |
| Code coverage data collector | `<DataCollector>` in `<DataCollectionRunSettings>` |

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- Global test timeout (ms) — mirrors TestTimeout from .testsettings -->
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <MSTest>
    <!-- Per-test method timeout (ms) -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- Mirrors the assembly-resolution / deployment behaviour -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>true</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.reasoningText:
The user has a `.testsettings` file with:
1. Assembly resolution / test deployment settings
2. A test timeout of 30000ms (30 seconds)
3. A code coverage data collector

They need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file, which is the modern format supported by MSTest v3+. The `.testsettings` format is legacy and no longer supported by newer MSTest versions.

Let me create a `.runsettings` file that captures all the settings from their `.testsettings` file.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- Global test timeout (ms) — mirrors TestTimeout from .testsettings -->
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <MSTest>
    <!-- Per-test method timeout (ms) -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- Mirrors the assembly-resolution / deployment behaviour -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>true</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content: Now wire it up in the `.csproj` so it's picked up automatically:

Here's what was created/changed:

**`local.runsettings`** (new) — replaces `local.testsettings` with the modern format:
- **Test timeout** → `<TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>` (per-test) + `<TestSessionTimeout>` (session-wide)
- **Deployment** → `<DeploymentEnabled>true</DeploymentEnabled>`
- **Code coverage** → `<DataCollector>` using the v2.0 collector URI

**`TestProject.csproj`** (updated) — added `<RunSettingsFilePath>` so the settings are auto-discovered by `dotnet test` and Visual Studio.

You can now safely delete `local.testsettings`. The old file is no longer read by MSTest v3+.
